info.description: This is a v1 release of the Alerts-Framework APIs.
info.title: Notifications

get.description: This API endpoint retrieves the list of Alerts based on the query parameters provided. The alerts list includes details such as name, type, category, severity, status, etc. The query parameters allow you to filter results that meet specific criteria, ensuring you receive only relevant data.

parameters.description:
OData Version 4.0 filter string (limited functionality). Supports only 'and' conjunction ('or' and 'not' are NOT supported). Supported fields and operators are in the table below. 

| Field | Operators |
| --------- | -------- |
| `siteId` | `eq` and `in` |
| `typeId` | `eq` and `in` |
| `status` | `eq` and `in` |
| `category` | `eq` and `in` |
| `deviceType` | `eq` and `in`|

**The operators are implemented as follows:**

* `eq`: Only values exactly matching the given value are returned.
* `in`: Only values present in the given list are returned.

**List of applicable filters for field and terms:**

| Field | Applicable Terms |
| --------- | -------- |
|  `siteId` | `string` |
| `typeId` | `string` |
| `status` | `["Active", "Deferred", "Cleared"]` |
| `deviceType` | `["Access Point", "Gateway", "Switch", "Bridge", "Access Point, Switch", "Switch, Switch"]` |
| `category` | `["Clients", "System", "LAN", "WLAN", "WAN", "Cluster", "Routing", "Security"]` |
parameters.name: filter

parameters.description: Sort field followed by a direction indicator ASC (ascending) or DESC (descending). Supported fields are `createdAt`,`severity`,`status`,`name`,`priority`. `createdAt` field is used by default.
parameters.name: sort

parameters.description: Specifies the pagination cursor for the next page of resources. Minimum value is 1.
parameters.name: next

parameters.description: Maximum number of alerts to be retrieved. Allowed range is 1 to 100.
parameters.name: limit

post.description: This API endpoint sets the specified alerts to active status. This can be used to reactivate deferred alerts.

get.description: This API endpoint retrieves the status of an asynchronous alert operation using the task ID returned from alert action endpoints (clear, defer, active, priority).

parameters.description: The unique task identifier returned from an alert action request.
parameters.name: task-id

get.description: This API endpoint retrieves the classification of alerts based on the specified type. The classification provides aggregated counts of alerts grouped by the specified criteria.

parameters.description:
The type of classification to retrieve. This determines how alerts are grouped. 
Possible values are `severity`, `status`, `priority`, `category`, `device_type`, `impacted_devices`
parameters.name: type

parameters.description: OData Version 4.0 filter string to filter the alerts before classification.
parameters.name: filter

parameters.description: Search string to filter alerts by name or summary before classification.
parameters.name: search

post.description: This API endpoint clears the specified alerts. Clearing an alert marks it as resolved with an optional reason and notes.
This API endpoint defers the specified alerts until a specified time. Deferred alerts will not generate notifications until the deferral period expires.
This API endpoint sets the priority level for the specified alerts.

get.description: Returns AI powered recommendations for optimizing performance. For site level granularity `site-id` and `id` filter shall be provided and for global recommendations `site-id` and `id` filter shall be omitted. At global level api returns global recommendations (across all sites) along with siteId and insightId.

parameters.description: Site identifier. Omit site-id to retrieve global recommendations across all sites. For site level recommendations site-id can be obtained from the same api from global recommendations through the field `siteId.
parameters.name: site-id

parameters.description: Insight identifier. Id is insightId which is used to filter recommendations, id can be obtained from the same api from global level recommendations through the field 'insightId'.
parameters.name: id
